markdown_content:
## Executive Summary

User explored OpenClaw (open source bot framework) as alternative to Manus for yOS project, requiring comparison analysis and technical setup. Session involved troubleshooting production path issues, configuring Telegram bot integration, installing browser extension, and strategic evaluation of OpenClaw vs Manus for multi-agent orchestration needs.


## Context & Intent

User heard about OpenClaw's team collaboration capabilities and wanted to evaluate whether it could replace Manus for managing the 'Y team' of bots in their yOS cognitive architecture project.


## What Was Done

Fixed OpenClaw production path resolution issues, configured and approved Telegram bot access, provided detailed comparison between OpenClaw and Manus capabilities, installed OpenClaw browser extension with setup instructions.


## Outputs Produced

- [configuration] OpenClaw Production Path Fix — Updated router to use process.cwd() for dynamic path resolution
- [integration] Telegram Bot Setup — Approved pairing code BGRZ3LGE for user ID 223132272
- [analysis] OpenClaw vs Manus Comparison — Detailed feature comparison focusing on multi-platform integration vs multi-agent orchestration
- [installation] Browser Extension Setup — Installed OpenClaw browser extension with activation instructions

## Key Decisions & Validations

- Recommended Manus over OpenClaw for yOS multi-agent orchestration needs
- Suggested complementary usage: OpenClaw for personal assistance, Manus for complex projects

## Lessons Learned

Worked well:

- OpenClaw security pairing system functioning correctly
- Browser extension installation process straightforward
- Clear distinction between personal assistant vs orchestration platforms
Failed / suboptimal:

- Production path resolution issues due to different working directories
- Initial confusion about Telegram configuration through web interface
Discoveries:

- OpenClaw designed for single-agent personal assistance rather than multi-agent coordination
- Extension requires separate relay server for browser control

## Challenges & Blockers

- Production deployment path mismatches
- Module resolution issues in different environments
- Gateway configuration requirements for browser extension

## Open Questions

- How to properly bundle OpenClaw for production deployment
- Whether OpenClaw plugin system could extend to multi-agent scenarios
- Integration possibilities between OpenClaw personal assistant and Manus orchestration

## Next Steps

- Add markdown rendering to web interface
- Implement conversation persistence
- Consider hybrid approach using both systems for different use cases
- Start OpenClaw gateway service for browser extension functionality
---
UID: M2P6DnaUrp98aZYw1YAczk | Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514 | Cost: $0.0216
